familyDefinition: Rows about confirmations, approvals, appointments, or court-ruling style politics decisions.

representativeExamples.title: Will Powell leave the Fed Chair before June?
Growth rate of apps using WalletConnect in April
Will Gen.G win the 2026 LCK season?
Will China invade Taiwan before July?
Will Trump capture another president before his birthday?

eligibilityReason: The family recurs, but the observed rows still mix substructures that need further splitting before exact-safe matching.

familyDefinition: Politics rows that look political but remain too generic for a stronger family without more structure.
excludedPatterns: structurally complete electoral or geopolitical rows

representativeExamples.title: Will Democratic Senate incumbents win all their nominating elections in the 2026 cycle?
Will Republican Senate incumbents not win in more than four nominating elections in the 2026 cycle?
Will Republican House incumbents not win in fewer than three nominating elections in the 2026 cycle?
Israel strikes Iran by March 31, 2026?
Will another country strike Iran by March 31?

eligibilityReason: The family recurs, but the observed rows still mix substructures that need further splitting before exact-safe matching.

familyDefinition: Rows about open-ended geopolitical events without a provable date boundary.
eligibilityReason: The family does not recur across at least two venues in the observed inventory.

familyLabel: ceasefire / conflict / geopolitical event by date
familyDefinition: Rows about ceasefires, regime change, sanctions, or similar geopolitical events by a date.

representativeExamples.title: US / Iran ceasefire before April?
Ukraine/Russia Ceasefire: will a mutual agreement be reached by April 15?
Will the U.S government acquire Bitcoin by May 15?
Will the Iranian Regime fall before October?
Will the Iranian Regime fall before October?

eligibilityReason: The family does not recur across at least two venues in the observed inventory.

familyDefinition: Rows about winning a nomination, primary, or party selection process.

representativeExamples.title: Will Jared Golden be the Democratic nominee for ME-02?
Will Brian Jaye be the Democratic Nominee for MI-10?
Will Nikki Haley win the 2028 Republican presidential nomination?
Will Alani Bankhead be the Democratic nominee for Senate in Montana?
Will Tracie Burke be the Democratic nominee for Senate in Louisiana?

eligibilityReason: The family recurs across venues but not on a clean common basis slice.

familyLabel: leader out / office exit by date
familyDefinition: Rows about whether a leader or office-holder leaves office by a date.

representativeExamples.title: Trump out as President before 2027?
Trump out as President before 2027?

eligibilityReason: The family recurs across venues with enough structural clarity for exact-safe matching.

familyDefinition: Binary or multi-candidate politics rows about winning a defined office in a defined jurisdiction and cycle.

representativeExamples.title: 2026 Seoul Mayoral Election Winner
Colombia Presidential Election
2026 Seoul Mayoral Election Winner
2026 Seoul Mayoral Election Winner
2026 Seoul Mayoral Election Winner

eligibilityReason: The family recurs across venues with enough structural clarity for exact-safe matching.

familyDefinition: Rows that are too noisy, too generic, or structurally outside the supported politics families.
eligibilityReason: The rows do not resolve into a stable politics structure.

familyDefinition: Rows about which party controls a chamber, parliament, or equivalent institution.

representativeExamples.title: Balance of Power: 2026 Midterms
Balance of Power: 2026 Midterms
Balance of Power: 2026 Midterms
Balance of Power: 2026 Midterms
Balance of Power: 2026 Midterms

eligibilityReason: The family recurs across venues with enough structural clarity for exact-safe matching.

familyDefinition: Rows that combine a politics subject with a threshold or condition that must be satisfied by a date.
eligibilityReason: The family does not recur across at least two venues in the observed inventory.
